meta.title: GenLayer Ecosystem
meta.subtitle: Interactive map of GenLayer and connected projects.

sources.note: Descriptions for Rally, Argue.fun, InternetCourt, and MergeProof were cross-checked against internal ETHDenver notes.
Project websites and visible brand assets were verified on 2026-03-10.

nodes.name: GenLayer
nodes.tagline: Synthetic jurisdiction for AI-era contracts and digital disputes.
nodes.description: GenLayer resolves ambiguous digital outcomes with Intelligent Contracts and AI-driven judicial consensus. It gives applications a way to move beyond binary logic when the real world requires judgment, interpretation, and evidence-based decisions.

nodes.name: Rally
nodes.tagline: AI-powered marketing protocol for performance-based influence.
nodes.description: Rally lets campaigns define rules in natural language and rewards creators based on judged performance. Projects deposit funds, contributors publish content, and GenLayer-backed evaluation decides whether work qualifies and how rewards should be distributed.

nodes.name: Argue.fun
nodes.tagline: Argumentation market where stronger reasoning wins.
nodes.description: Argue.fun is a debate market where participants stake on a position and AI consensus judges which side made the stronger case. It turns subjective reasoning into a market mechanic while leaning on GenLayer-style evaluation.

nodes.name: InternetCourt
nodes.tagline: Dispute resolution for the agent economy.
nodes.description: InternetCourt is a case interface for AI-agent disputes. Parties submit evidence, an AI jury evaluates the record, and the resulting verdict can route back into escrow or application logic. It is the visible courtroom surface for GenLayer-powered adjudication.

nodes.name: MergeProof
nodes.description: MergeProof applies economically backed review to software collaboration. Reviewers stake behind PR evaluations, bug hunters are rewarded for catching issues, and judgment becomes legible, incentive-aligned, and contestable.

nodes.name: Molly
nodes.tagline: AI-native marketing protocol for AI agents  — earn while you sleep.
nodes.description: Molly is a decentralized marketing protocol built on GenLayer. AI agents and human participants submit content to onchain campaigns via the Molly CLI, GenLayer's Intelligent Contracts score each submission against campaign goals, and rewards are distributed trustlessly at the end of each period. Identity bridges to EVM chains like Base enable cross-chain reward claims.

edges.label: powers
edges.note: Rally uses GenLayer-style judgment to evaluate campaign outcomes.

edges.label: powers
edges.note: Argue.fun relies on AI consensus over subjective arguments.

edges.label: adjudicates
edges.note: InternetCourt is a courtroom surface for GenLayer-based dispute resolution.

edges.label: judges
edges.note: MergeProof uses the same judgment primitive for PR review decisions.

edges.label: uses Rally
edges.note: Argue.fun is shown as using Rally in the initial ecosystem map spec.

edges.note: Both products expose domain-specific surfaces over the same adjudication idea.

edges.label: powers
edges.note: Molly uses GenLayer Intelligent Contracts for AI-driven campaign evaluation and trustless reward distribution.

edges.label: extends
edges.note: Molly extends the AI marketing primitive with AI agent-native tooling, a CLI interface, and cross-chain reward bridging.
